findings.message: Duplicate data-access layer: routes import from both `item_service` (add_item, fetch_all_items) and previously used `repository` (list_items, create_item), creating two parallel implementations of the same data operations
findings.suggestion: Consolidate all data-access functions into a single module. Either extend `repository.py` with the `item_service` functions or migrate all functions to `item_service.py`, then update imports across the codebase to use the unified layer.

findings.message: Transaction boundary management in route handler: the POST handler directly calls `session.commit()` and `session.refresh(item)` instead of delegating to the data-access layer. The data-access function `add_item()` only performs `flush()`, forcing the route to complete the transaction.
findings.suggestion: Move transaction finalization into the data-access layer. Either: (1) modify `add_item()` to perform commit and refresh itself, or (2) use the existing `repository.create_item()` function which already handles these operations. Keep transaction boundaries within the data layer, not the route handler.
